data.title: New Zealand Shares Tumble as Asian Peers Lack Direction; Chorus Falls Nearly 5% on Fiscal H1 Loss
data.description:
New Zealand shares fell sharply on Monday led by a decline in health services stocks, while Asian peers lacked direction amid a mixed trade.

The S&P/NZX 50 Index fell 1.7%, or 220.86 points, to close at 12,531.72.

Health services stocks were the main losers, followed by distribution services and electronic technology.

In Asia, Hong Kong's Hang Seng lost 0.6%, while the Shanghai SSE was little changed. Japanese markets were closed for the Emperor's Birthday.

In domestic news, the total volume of seasonally adjusted retail sales in New Zealand rose 0.9% to NZ$24.72 billion in the December 2024 quarter, following a flat movement in the prior three-month period, per Stats NZ data.

Credit card spending rose 1.3% month on month to NZ$4.62 billion in January following a 1.1% increase in December 2024, according to the Reserve Bank.

In corporate news, Chorus fell almost 5% after it reported a loss per diluted share of NZ$0.02 in the fiscal first half, compared with earnings of NZ$0.01 per diluted share a year earlier.

Steel & Tube Holdings was up past 2% even though it logged a fiscal first-half diluted loss of NZ$0.062 per share, compared with earnings of NZ$0.031 per share a year earlier.

Elsewhere, Ryman Healthcare launched a NZ$1 billion equity raise to "reset the balance sheet," comprising a NZ$313 million institutional placement and a NZ$688 million pro-rata accelerated non-renounceable entitlement offer. The company requested a trading halt Monday.

data.title: Storj to Participate in ETHDenver 2025 in Denver on February 23rd
data.description:
Storj is set to participate in ETHDenver 2025, one of the largest gatherings for blockchain and Web3 innovators. The event will occur in Denver, from February 23rd to March 2nd.

Refer to the official tweet by STORJ:

Storj
@storj

@storj is heading to ETHDenver 2025 in 2 DAYS! 🚀 Join us Feb 23rd - Mar 2nd at one of the biggest gatherings for blockchain and Web3 innovators! Connect with developers, founders, and investors pushing the boundaries of decentralizationâœ¨🔗Get Tickets: https://t.co/iBrBX901LY pic.twitter.com/1fegUpABGj

Feb 21, 2025

STORJ Info

Storj is an open-source platform developed by Storj Labs Inc., aiming to revolutionize cloud storage through blockchain technology. Offering end-to-end encrypted services, Storj distinguishes itself from traditional cloud storage solutions by emphasizing speed, cost-efficiency, and enhanced security. Instead of relying on centralized data centers, Storj operates using a decentralized peer-to-peer network where individuals share their storage capacities.

The operational foundation of Storj revolves around its decentralized approach, harnessing the storage capacities of a broad network of peers. This structure ensures data integrity, rapid retrieval, and robust security. Through this unique mechanism, Storj eliminates the common vulnerabilities associated with centralized data centers and offers a more resilient and efficient storage alternative.

Central to Storj’s ecosystem is the STORJ token. Rather than adopting a mining approach for token generation, Storj Labs opted to pre-mine all its tokens at the outset, ensuring a fixed supply. Users can earn STORJ tokens by offering their storage as “farmers” on the network. Moreover, the tokens are available for trading on cryptocurrency exchanges.

data.title: JES International to Delist on Feb. 26
data.description:
JES International said it will be delisted from the Singapore Exchange Securities Trading on Feb. 26, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Saturday.

Following the delisting, the company will continue to exist as an unlisted public limited company, the filing added.

data.title: Hong Kong investment firm’s board gives nod to more Bitcoin buying
data.description:
HK Asia Holdings Limited has increased its Bitcoin holdings to nearly 9 BTC, just a week after the investment firm saw its share prices double after buying its first Bitcoin.

HK Asia, a Hong Kong-based investment firm, said in a Feb. 23 announcement that its board “has approved the Company to further increase its investment in Bitcoin” and shared that on Feb. 20, it purchased around 7.88 Bitcoin for a total cost of around $761,705.

It added that its latest Bitcoin buy was financed through internal resources and brought its total Bitcoin holdings to around 8.88 BTC, which it bought at an average cost of $97,021 per coin — around $861,500 in total.

On Feb. 16, HK Asia shared it purchased 1 BTC, which was enough for investors to pile into its stock the next day when markets reopened to boost its share price by nearly 93% by the close of trading on Feb. 17.

Shares in HK Asia were up by around 5.7% on Feb. 24 as of the lunch break on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and were trading at around 6.66 Hong Kong dollars (86 cents), according to Google Finance.

If its current price holds, HK Asia’s stock could be set to close above its June 2019 all-time high of 6.50 Hong Kong dollars (84 cents), with its share price already up 1,700% so far this year.

HK Asia’s Bitcoin foray follows a recent trend of publicly traded firms buying the cryptocurrency with the aim of boosting company earnings.

In its announcement earlier this month, HK Asia said its board had seen the “increasing popularity of cryptocurrencies in the commercial world” as part of the reason for its initial BTC purchase.

The company said in its latest note that its Bitcoin buys are below the threshold for it to legally have to share its bought the cryptocurrency but made the announcement “on a voluntary basis.”

Bitcoin has traded flat over the past 24 hours and is currently down less than 1% to $95,537. It has traded below the psychologically important $100,000 level since Feb. 5 and is down 12% from its Jan. 20 peak of $108,786, according to CoinGecko.

data.title: Ankr to Hold AMA on X on February 27th
data.description:
Ankr will host an AMA on X featuring Kava on February 27th at 16:00 UTC. The event will focus on Kava’s efforts to connect these two major blockchain ecosystems.

ANKR Info

Ankr is a Web3 decentralized infrastructure provider that helps developers, dapps, and stakers easily interact with multiple blockchains. It allows you to create DApps using API and RPC, staking on Ankr Earn, and use customized blockchain solutions for businesses.

Ankr uses computing power supported by DLT to collect data resources that are deployed to stakers and developers through data centers at lower costs, which is how developers and stakers can easily create and deploy dApps and stake their balances.

The Ankr project also offers Stkr, a service that allows users to run their own Ethereum 2.0 nodes and earn rewards through staking. This enables users to participate in the Ethereum 2.0 network without the need to independently manage all the technical aspects.

ANKR is the native token of the Ankr project, used to participate in network management voting, pay for platform premium services, and for staking.

data.title: Soccer-PSV sign former Arsenal striker Perez ahead of their Champions League tie
data.description:
PSV Eindhoven have signed former Arsenal striker Lucas Perez until the end of the season, the Dutch club said on Sunday, just over a week before they host the London club in the first leg of their Champions League round of 16 tie.

The 36-year-old joined PSV as a free agent after leaving his hometown club Deportivo La Coruna last month.

Perez joined Arsenal in 2016 from Deportivo for a reported fee of 20 million euros ($21.02 million), but found limited success at the Premier League side. He returned to Deportivo on loan the following year before joining West Ham United in 2018.

"I'm tremendously driven to help this club achieve its other goals, including winning the national title and national cup," Perez said in a statement.

"I've worked extremely hard in recent weeks, both on and off the pitch. I'm more than ready to prove myself here."

($1 = 0.9513 euros)

data.title: WiseTech Responds to Australian Securities Exchange's Query Letter Over Richard White's Role, Shares Down 20%
data.description:
WiseTech Global said Richard White operates in the company under terms disclosed by the company on Oct. 24 2024, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing in response to the Australian Securities Exchange's query letter.

According to the announcement on Oct. 24 2024, White works under the title of founder and founding chief executive officer and is a consultant to the board focused on product and business development.

This comes after four of the company's directors, including the chairman, resigned earlier today due to "intractable differences and differing views" around the ongoing role of Richard White.

Company shares dropped 20% in recent trade.

data.title: Percheron Therapeutics Submits Non-binding Licensing Proposal for Drug  Development Program, Shares Jump 8%
data.description:
Percheron Therapeutics submitted a non-binding proposal to a pharmaceutical company to in-license a new drug development program focusing on a rare neurological disease, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

The proposal remains subject to further negotiations, as well as the execution of a definitive agreement, the filing said.

The firm estimated that at this stage it would take eight to 12 weeks to negotiate and sign a definitive agreement.

It plans to continue to evaluate alternative opportunities in parallel to the proposal.

The firm's shares jumped 8% in recent Monday trade.

data.title: Anyuan Coal Industry Group Subsidiary Establishes Jiangxi Luoshi Mining; Shares Fall 7%
data.description:
Anyuan Coal Industry Group subsidiary Jiangxi Coal Group established Jiangxi Luoshi Mining with a registered capital of 120 million yuan.

Jiangxi Luoshi Mining aims to enhance coal mining operations, provide technical consulting and develop new energy solutions, according to a Feb. 22 filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The newly established company still needs to go through industrial and commercial approval registration procedures.

Shares of Anyuan Coal Industry Group were down 7% in recent trade.

data.title: Sanofi, Teva Pharmaceuticals Inflammatory Bowel Diseases' Drug Trial Shows Positive Results
data.description:
Sanofi and Teva Pharmaceuticals on Feb. 22 reported positive results from the Relieve UCCD phase 2b trial of duvakitug, a medication for the two most common forms of inflammatory bowel diseases, ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease.

For moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis, 48% of patients treated with duvakitug achieved the primary endpoint of clinical remission, compared with 20% on placebo. Meanwhile, 26% of patients with Crohn's disease achieved the primary endpoint of endoscopic response in the trial, higher than 13% on placebo.

The French pharmaceutical company and its partner, an affiliate of Teva Pharmaceutical Industries, reported that the medication was generally well tolerated, with no new safety signals identified.

data.title: Capital Economics says USD/JPY expected to reach 145 by year-end 2025
data.description:
Japanese inflation and PMI data support expectations for rising JGB yields, says Capital Economics' John Higgins.

  • The 10-year JGB yield is forecast to hit 1.75% by end-2025, driven by investor reassessments of the BOJ’s policy outlook, with rates likely reaching 1.25% by 2026.
  • Stronger yields could also boost the yen, with USD/JPY expected to reach 145 by year-end 2025, according to Capital Economics.

Infor comes via a Wall Street Journal report (gated)

data.title: Industrial Bank to Acquire Parts of Shenzhen Property for Up to 6.6 Billion Yuan
data.description:
Industrial Bank said its Shenzhen branch will acquire portions of a commercial property in the T208-0054 plot in Nanshan District, Shenzhen, for up to 6.6 billion yuan.

The property is an 82-story building with eight underground levels, set for completion in 2029, according to a Feb. 21 filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The acquisition is expected to support the bank's operations and enhance its strategic presence in the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area.

data.title: GIH Unit Receives Regulatory Approval for Stake Acquisition
data.description:
Gulf Investment House (ADX:GIH), or GIH, subsidiary Bait Al-Amar Al-Khaleeji secured approval from the Competition Protection Agency for the economic concentration process related to acquiring a 54.84% stake in Energy House Holding.

The Abu Dhabi-listed financial services company said Sunday it is completing the contracting requirements, which are subject to the seller's approval.

data.title: Palestine Trade Gap Largest in 4 Months
data.description:
Palestine’s trade deficit widened to USD 434.4 million in December 2024, the largest since August and up from USD 326.6 million in the corresponding month of the previous year, as exports fell further and imports rose.

Exports plunged 27% year-over-year to USD 164.2 million, as growth in outbound shipments to Israel slowed to 35% from a 68% increase in November, accounting for 84% of total exports.

Shipments to other countries also continued to decline, down 2%, though less severe than the previous 12% drop.

Meanwhile, imports surged 31% to USD 598.6 million, driven by a 17% rise in purchases from Israel, which represented 55% of total imports, and a 55% increase in imports from other countries.

For the full year, the trade deficit narrowed to USD 4,817.9 million from USD 6,183.4 million the previous year.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 10:00 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.94% at 74,600, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 declined 0.95% to 22,579 mirroring global peers, as concerns over slowing U.S. growth added to ongoing worries over the impact of President Donald Trump's tariffs.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose 0.1% versus the U.S. dollar to 86.64, boosted by the drop in the dollar and U.S. Treasury yields after economic data fuelled worries over the U.S. growth outlook.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 100.60 rupees, with the yield down marginally at 6.7029%, tracking the fall in U.S. yields.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down 2 basis points at 6.28%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) dropped 4 bps to 6.04%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 6.40%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.20%.

data.title: Zhujiang Brewery's Profit Jumps 30% in 2024; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Guangzhou Zhujiang Brewery posted a net profit attributable to shareholders of 812.6 million yuan in 2024, up 30% from 623.5 million yuan a year earlier, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Saturday.

Earnings per share climbed 32% year on year to 0.37 yuan from 0.28 yuan.

The Chinese brewery generated 5.73 billion yuan in operating income, up 6.6% from 5.38 billion yuan in the previous year.

The company's shares jumped less than 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Deep Blue Aerospace's IPO Timing Will Depend on Product Development, Capital-Market Conditions, CEO Says
Deep Blue Aerospace Aims to Conduct Orbital Flight Test of Reusable Rocket Nebula-1 in June or July, CEO Says
China's Leading Private Rocket Company Has Eye on Listing in 2028 — Interview
data.description:
(04:33 GMT) *China's Deep Blue Aerospace Eyes Public Listing in 2028, CEO Says
(04:33 GMT) *Deep Blue Aerospace's IPO Timing Will Depend on Product Development, Capital-Market Conditions, CEO Says
(04:33 GMT) *Deep Blue Aerospace Aims to Conduct Orbital Flight Test of Reusable Rocket Nebula-1 in June or July, CEO Says
(04:33 GMT) *China Can Catch Up to SpaceX in 2030 in Optimistic Scenario, Deep Blue Aerospace CEO Says
(04:33 GMT) China's Leading Private Rocket Company Has Eye on Listing in 2028 — Interview

By Clarence Leong

Deep Blue Aerospace, one of China's leading rocket companies, said it expects to meet the conditions for an initial public offering as soon as 2028.

The chief executive of the Jiangsu-based company said in an interview with The Wall Street Journal that while it intends to go public in the future, the timing will depend on the company's product development and China's capital-market conditions.

"Reusable rockets are a disruptive technology that will have huge market potential in the future," said Huo Liang, CEO of Deep Blue Aerospace.

Founded in 2016, the company is developing rockets that will be key to China's pursuit of its own Starlink-like satellite internet network. Deep Blue Aerospace's latest valuation is 5 billion yuan, equivalent to about $690 million, according to the company. It raised close to $200 million in funds last year, it said.

Reusable rockets--pioneered by Elon Musk's SpaceX--can dramatically lower the cost of accessing space. Deep Blue Aerospace plans to conduct an orbital flight and recovery test of its Nebula-1 rocket around June or July this year, Huo said. The rocket is designed to carry up to a 2-ton payload to low-Earth orbit, according to the company's website.

Huo, who worked for one of China's major space contractors before founding Deep Blue Aerospace, said SpaceX is far ahead of competitors because it has achieved frequent and stable recovery of its rocket boosters. However, he said that in an optimistic scenario, China could catch up to SpaceX by 2030.

"The industry's development in China isn't linear, and it'll certainly accelerate in coming years," Huo said. "More and more of the key elements are falling in place, just like pieces of a puzzle."

Besides rocket launches, Deep Blue Aerospace aims to operate its first commercial suborbital flight in 2027. Huo appeared in a livestream last year to sell a pair of tickets to the inaugural flight, with each ticket going for 1 million yuan after a discount. The tickets were snapped up.

Huo said Beijing's emphasis on the commercial space sector has had a positive impact on fundraising. Last year, the government's work report mentioned commercial spaceflight as one of the "new growth engines" for the first time.

A handful of Chinese companies plan to test reusable rockets this year, including Beijing Tianbing Technology, which is targeting June for the maiden flight of Tianlong-3. Huo said he expects a few rocket models to be in commercial operation by next year.

Write to Clarence Leong at clarence.leong@wsj.com
